title: Desired-state envelope (v1alpha1)
description: Signed, replay-protected desired-state document the Hub publishes and the Operator reconciles. TARGET-STATE contract. There is NO arbitrary shell: only scoped capability intents. Carries NO secret values. Approved crypto (ADR-0002): Hub control-plane messages are signed with ed25519 (keys in KMS/HSM); Sigstore is NOT required and public transparency logs are NOT relied on for normal Hub->Operator operation; age is never accepted here.

revision.description: Monotonic revision; the Operator ignores a revision lower than its last applied.

signature.description: Ed25519 detached signature over the canonicalised document. Sigstore/cosign and age are rejected for the control plane; the algorithm is constrained to ed25519 and a trust_domain is required so the Operator resolves the Hub key offline.

intents.description: Declarative, scoped capability intents. The Operator reconciles the delta; it never runs free-form commands.

capability.description: Bounded capability. Enumerated, never a shell.

target.description: Scoped target the capability applies to (package id, service name, secret reference, area slug).

params.description: Capability-specific parameters. Never contains secret values, only references.

requires_superadmin_approval.description: High-impact intents (data migration, critical secret rotation, major upgrade) require explicit approval before apply. See ADR-0007.
